1: require "json"
2: require "securerandom"
3: require "faraday"
4: require "uri"
5: require "digest"
6: 
7: module Raix
8:   module MCP
9:     # Client for communicating with MCP servers via Server-Sent Events (SSE).
10:     class SseClient
11:       PROTOCOL_VERSION = "2024-11-05".freeze
12:       CONNECTION_TIMEOUT = 10
13:       OPEN_TIMEOUT = 30
14: 
15:       # Creates a new client and establishes SSE connection to discover the JSON-RPC endpoint.
16:       #
17:       # @param url [String] the SSE endpoint URL
18:       def initialize(url, headers: {})
19:         @url = url
20:         @endpoint_url = nil
21:         @sse_thread = nil
22:         @event_queue = Thread::Queue.new
23:         @buffer = ""
24:         @closed = false
25:         @headers = headers
26: 
27:         # Start the SSE connection and discover endpoint
28:         establish_sse_connection
29:       end
30: 
31:       # Returns available tools from the server.
32:       def tools
33:         @tools ||= begin
34:           request_id = SecureRandom.uuid
35:           send_json_rpc(request_id, "tools/list", {})
36: 
37:           # Wait for response through SSE
38:           response = wait_for_response(request_id)
39:           response[:tools].map do |tool_json|
40:             Tool.from_json(tool_json)
41:           end
42:         end
43:       end
44: 
45:       # Executes a tool with given arguments.
46:       # Returns text content directly, or JSON-encoded data for other content types.
47:       def call_tool(name, **arguments)
48:         request_id = SecureRandom.uuid
49:         send_json_rpc(request_id, "tools/call", name:, arguments:)
50: 
51:         # Wait for response through SSE
52:         response = wait_for_response(request_id)
53:         content = response[:content]
54:         return "" if content.nil? || content.empty?
55: 
56:         # Handle different content formats
57:         first_item = content.first
58:         case first_item
59:         when Hash
60:           case first_item[:type]
61:           when "text"
62:             first_item[:text]
63:           when "image"
64:             # Return a structured response for images
65:             {
66:               type: "image",
67:               data: first_item[:data],
68:               mime_type: first_item[:mimeType] || "image/png"
69:             }.to_json
70:           else
71:             # For any other type, return the item as JSON
72:             first_item.to_json
73:           end
74:         else
75:           first_item.to_s
76:         end
77:       end
78: 
79:       # Closes the connection to the server.
80:       def close
81:         @closed = true
82:         @sse_thread&.kill
83:         @connection&.close
84:       end
85: 
86:       def unique_key
87:         parametrized_url = @url.parameterize.underscore.gsub("https_", "")
88:         Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(parametrized_url)[0..2]
89:       end
90: 
91:       private
92: 
93:       # Establishes and maintains the SSE connection
94:       def establish_sse_connection
95:         @sse_thread = Thread.new do
96:           headers = {
97:             "Accept" => "text/event-stream",
98:             "Cache-Control" => "no-cache",
99:             "Connection" => "keep-alive",
100:             "MCP-Version" => PROTOCOL_VERSION
101:           }.merge(@headers)
102: 
103:           @connection = Faraday.new(url: @url) do |faraday|
104:             faraday.options.timeout = CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
105:             faraday.options.open_timeout = OPEN_TIMEOUT
106:           end
107: 
108:           @connection.get do |req|
109:             req.headers = headers
110:             req.options.on_data = proc do |chunk, _size|
111:               next if @closed
112: 
113:               @buffer << chunk
114:               process_sse_buffer
115:             end
116:           end
117:         rescue StandardError => e
118:           # puts "[MCP DEBUG] SSE connection error: #{e.message}"
119:           @event_queue << { error: e }
120:         end
121: 
122:         # Wait for endpoint discovery
123:         loop do
124:           event = @event_queue.pop
125:           if event[:error]
126:             raise ProtocolError, "SSE connection failed: #{event[:error].message}"
127:           elsif event[:endpoint_url]
128:             @endpoint_url = event[:endpoint_url]
129:             break
130:           end
131:         end
132: 
133:         # Initialize the MCP session
134:         initialize_mcp_session
135:       end
136: 
137:       # Process SSE buffer for complete events
138:       def process_sse_buffer
139:         while (idx = @buffer.index("\n\n"))
140:           event_text = @buffer.slice!(0..(idx + 1))
141:           event_type, event_data = parse_sse_fields(event_text)
142: 
143:           case event_type
144:           when "endpoint"
145:             endpoint_url = build_absolute_url(@url, event_data)
146:             @event_queue << { endpoint_url: }
147:           when "message"
148:             handle_message_event(event_data)
149:           end
150:         end
151:       end
152: 
153:       # Handle SSE message events
154:       def handle_message_event(event_data)
155:         parsed = JSON.parse(event_data, symbolize_names: true)
156: 
157:         # Handle different message types
158:         case parsed
159:         when ->(p) { p[:method] == "initialize" && p.dig(:params, :endpoint_url) }
160:           # Legacy endpoint discovery
161:           endpoint_url = parsed.dig(:params, :endpoint_url)
162:           @event_queue << { endpoint_url: }
163:         when ->(p) { p[:id] && p[:result] }
164:           @event_queue << { id: parsed[:id], result: parsed[:result] }
165:         when ->(p) { p[:result] }
166:           @event_queue << { result: parsed[:result] }
167:         end
168:       rescue JSON::ParserError => e
169:         puts "[MCP DEBUG] Error parsing message: #{e.message}"
170:         puts "[MCP DEBUG] Message data: #{event_data}"
171:       end
172: 
173:       # Initialize the MCP session
174:       def initialize_mcp_session
175:         request_id = SecureRandom.uuid
176:         send_json_rpc(request_id, "initialize", {
177:                         protocolVersion: PROTOCOL_VERSION,
178:                         capabilities: {
179:                           roots: { listChanged: true },
180:                           sampling: {}
181:                         },
182:                         clientInfo: {
183:                           name: "Raix",
184:                           version: Raix::VERSION
185:                         }
186:                       })
187: 
188:         # Wait for initialization response
189:         response = wait_for_response(request_id)
190: 
191:         # Send acknowledgment if needed
192:         return unless response.dig(:capabilities, :tools, :listChanged)
193: 
194:         send_notification("notifications/initialized", {})
195:       end
196: 
197:       # Send a JSON-RPC request
198:       def send_json_rpc(id, method, params)
199:         body = {
200:           jsonrpc: JSONRPC_VERSION,
201:           id:,
202:           method:,
203:           params:
204:         }
205: 
206:         # Use a new connection for the POST request
207:         conn = Faraday.new(url: @endpoint_url) do |faraday|
208:           faraday.options.timeout = CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
209:         end
210: 
211:         conn.post do |req|
212:           req.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json"
213:           req.body = body.to_json
214:         end
215:       rescue StandardError => e
216:         raise ProtocolError, "Failed to send request: #{e.message}"
217:       end
218: 
219:       # Send a notification (no response expected)
220:       def send_notification(method, params)
221:         body = {
222:           jsonrpc: JSONRPC_VERSION,
223:           method:,
224:           params:
225:         }
226: 
227:         conn = Faraday.new(url: @endpoint_url) do |faraday|
228:           faraday.options.timeout = CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
229:         end
230: 
231:         conn.post do |req|
232:           req.headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json"
233:           req.body = body.to_json
234:         end
235:       rescue StandardError => e
236:         puts "[MCP DEBUG] Error sending notification: #{e.message}"
237:       end
238: 
239:       # Wait for a response with a specific ID
240:       def wait_for_response(request_id)
241:         timeout = Time.now + CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
242: 
243:         loop do
244:           if Time.now > timeout
245:             raise ProtocolError, "Timeout waiting for response"
246:           end
247: 
248:           # Use non-blocking pop with timeout
249:           begin
250:             event = @event_queue.pop(true) # non_block = true
251:           rescue ThreadError
252:             # Queue is empty, wait a bit
253:             sleep 0.1
254:             next
255:           end
256: 
257:           if event[:error]
258:             raise ProtocolError, "SSE error: #{event[:error].message}"
259:           elsif event[:result] && (event[:id] == request_id || !event[:id])
260:             return event[:result]
261:           else
262:             @event_queue << event
263:             sleep 0.01
264:           end
265:         end
266:       end
267: 
268:       # Parses SSE event fields from raw text.
269:       def parse_sse_fields(event_text)
270:         event_type = "message"
271:         data_lines = []
272: 
273:         event_text.each_line do |line|
274:           case line
275:           when /^event:\s*(.+)$/
276:             event_type = Regexp.last_match(1).strip
277:           when /^data:\s*(.*)$/
278:             data_lines << Regexp.last_match(1)
279:           end
280:         end
281: 
282:         [event_type, data_lines.join("\n").strip]
283:       end
284: 
285:       # Builds an absolute URL for candidate relative to base.
286:       def build_absolute_url(base, candidate)
287:         uri = URI.parse(candidate)
288:         return candidate if uri.absolute?
289: 
290:         URI.join(base, candidate).to_s
291:       rescue URI::InvalidURIError
292:         candidate
293:       end
294:     end
295:   end
296: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "json"
4: 
5: # Test MCP Server implementing the Model Context Protocol over stdio transport
6: # This server provides several test tools for validating the StdioClient functionality
7: class TestMCPServer
8:   JSONRPC_VERSION = "2.0"
9: 
10:   def initialize
11:     $stdout.sync = true # Enable auto-flushing for immediate output
12:     @tools = build_tools
13:   end
14: 
15:   def run
16:     # Read JSON-RPC requests from stdin and respond on stdout
17:     while (line = $stdin.gets)
18:       begin
19:         request = JSON.parse(line.strip)
20:         response = handle_request(request)
21:         puts response.to_json if response
22:       rescue JSON::ParserError => e
23:         error_response = create_error_response(nil, -32_700, "Parse error: #{e.message}")
24:         puts error_response.to_json
25:       rescue StandardError => e
26:         error_response = create_error_response(request&.dig("id"), -32_603, "Internal error: #{e.message}")
27:         puts error_response.to_json
28:       end
29:     end
30:   end
31: 
32:   private
33: 
34:   def create_response(id:, result: nil, error: nil)
35:     {
36:       jsonrpc: JSONRPC_VERSION,
37:       id:,
38:       result:,
39:       error:
40:     }.compact
41:   end
42: 
43:   def create_error_response(id, code, message)
44:     create_response(id:, error: { code:, message: })
45:   end
46: 
47:   def handle_request(request)
48:     method = request["method"]
49:     params = request["params"] || {}
50:     id = request["id"]
51: 
52:     case method
53:     when "tools/list"
54:       handle_tools_list(id)
55:     when "tools/call"
56:       handle_tools_call(id, params)
57:     else
58:       create_error_response(id, -32_601, "Method not found: #{method}")
59:     end
60:   end
61: 
62:   def handle_tools_list(id)
63:     tools_without_handlers = @tools.values.map do |tool|
64:       tool.except("handler")
65:     end
66:     create_response(id:, result: { tools: tools_without_handlers })
67:   end
68: 
69:   def handle_tools_call(id, params)
70:     tool_name = params["name"]
71:     arguments = params["arguments"] || {}
72: 
73:     tool = @tools[tool_name]
74:     unless tool
75:       return create_error_response(id, -32_602, "Unknown tool: #{tool_name}")
76:     end
77: 
78:     begin
79:       content = tool["handler"].call(arguments)
80:       create_response(id:, result: { content: })
81:     rescue ArgumentError => e
82:       create_error_response(id, -32_602, "Invalid parameters: #{e.message}")
83:     end
84:   end
85:

87:     {
88:       "ping" => {

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Raix
4:   # Context object passed to before_completion hooks.
5:   # Provides access to the chat completion instance, messages, and request parameters.
6:   # Messages can be mutated for content filtering, PII redaction, etc.
7:   class CompletionContext
8:     attr_reader :chat_completion, :messages, :params
9: 
10:     def initialize(chat_completion:, messages:, params:)
11:       @chat_completion = chat_completion
12:       @messages = messages # mutable - hooks can modify for filtering, redaction, etc.
13:       @params = params # mutable - hooks can modify parameters
14:     end
15: 
16:     # Convenience accessor for the transcript
17:     def transcript
18:       chat_completion.transcript
19:     end
20: 
21:     # Get the currently configured model
22:     def current_model
23:       chat_completion.model || chat_completion.configuration.model
24:     end
25: 
26:     # Get the class that includes ChatCompletion
27:     def chat_completion_class
28:       chat_completion.class
29:     end
30: 
31:     # Get the current configuration
32:     def configuration
33:       chat_completion.configuration

Raix (Ruby AI eXtensions) is a Ruby gem built on RubyLLM that adds LLM chat-completion via mixins. Core tool-calling machinery:

- Entry/chat loop: `lib/raix/chat_completion.rb:39` (concern) / `#chat_completion` at `:73`
- Function declaration DSL: `lib/raix/function_dispatch.rb:23` (concern) / `#function` at `:46`
- Function → RubyLLM tool schema: `lib/raix/function_tool_adapter.rb:5`
- Transcript storage/conversion: `lib/raix/transcript_adapter.rb:5`
- Message format conversion (OpenAI shape): `lib/raix/message_adapters/base.rb:7`
- Multimodal content handling: `lib/raix/multimodal_content_adapter.rb:19`
- External tool integration (MCP): `lib/raix/mcp.rb:26`
- MCP tool representation: `lib/raix/mcp/tool.rb:11`
- MCP transports: `lib/raix/mcp/stdio_client.rb:8`, `lib/raix/mcp/sse_client.rb:10`
- Hooks/context: `lib/raix/completion_context.rb:7`, `lib/raix/configuration.rb:5`
- Tests live under `spec/raix/`.

## Step 2: Trace a tool-call round-trip

1. Entry: `ChatCompletion#chat_completion` `lib/raix/chat_completion.rb:73`.
2. Message normalization: adapter created at `:133`; every transcript message transformed at `:139` via `MessageAdapters::Base#transform` `lib/raix/message_adapters/base.rb:17`.
3. Request sent: `ruby_llm_request` `lib/raix/chat_completion.rb:147`.
4. In `ruby_llm_request` (`:340`), messages are applied to RubyLLM chat `:349-372`; tools converted via `FunctionToolAdapter.convert_tools_for_ruby_llm` at `:382-385`.
5. Response parsed for tool calls at `chat_completion.rb:160`.
6. Authorization check: function name must exist in `self.class.functions` at `:184`.
7. Dispatch: `dispatch_tool_function` at `:186`; default implementation at `:297` calls `public_send(function_name, arguments, cache)`.
8. Function body executes and appends assistant+tool messages to transcript in `FunctionDispatch#function` (`lib/raix/function_dispatch.rb:67-106`, transcript append at `:80-101`).
9. Loop decision: reads updated transcript, checks last message role/tool_calls at `chat_completion.rb:195-198`; if more tool calls needed, recurses `chat_completion` at `:200-209`.
10. Final text response extracted at `:222-224` and appended to transcript.

- RubyLLM message assembly: `ruby_llm_request` `:349-372` applies system (`:355`), user (`:356-358`), assistant (`:359-364`), and tool (`:365-371`) roles.
